Italy

Italy

United Kingdom

United Kingdom

Multilateralism

Multilateralism

Déclaration conjointe

On : March 08th 2025

We, the Foreign Ministers of France, Germany, Italy and the United Kingdom welcome the Arab initiative of a Recovery and Reconstruction Plan for Gaza. The plan shows a realistic path to the reconstruction of Gaza and promises – if implemented – swift and sustainable improvement of the catastrophic living conditions for the Palestinians living in Gaza. Recovery and reconstruction efforts must be based upon a solid political and security framework acceptable to both Israelis and Palestinians, which provides long term peace and security for both Israelis and Palestinians alike. We are clear that Hamas must neither govern Gazanor be a threat to Israel any more. We explicitly support the central role for the Palestinian Authority and the implementation of its reform agenda.

We commend the serious efforts of all involved stakeholders and appreciate the important signal the Arab states have sent by jointly developing this recovery and reconstruction plan. We are committed to working with the Arab initiative, the Palestinians and Israel to address those issues together, including security and governance. We urge all parties to build on the plan’s merits as a starting point

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Gaza reconstruction plan adopted by the Arab League
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Gaza reconstruction plan adopted by the Arab League

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Multilateralism

Multilateralism

On : March 06th 2025

France hails the proposed Gaza reconstruction plan formulated by the Arab League countries at the Cairo summit on March 4.

This plan offers a serious, credible basis for immediately meeting reconstruction, governance and security needs in the aftermath of the war in Gaza. It guarantees the respect of international law and maintains Gaza’s future within the framework of a future Palestinian State.

This plan must entirely exclude Hamas from the governance of Gaza, where it must be disarmed, while providing Israel with solid security guarantees.

This June, France and Saudi Arabia will co-chair an international conference for the implementation of the two-state solution. That will enable us to provide material support for this Arab plan and for the establishment of a Palestinian state living side-by-side with Israel in peace and security.

Q: What is France’s position on U.S. President Donald Trump’s recent statements on moving Palestinians to Egypt and neighboring countries?

A: France’s position has not changed: any forced displacement of Gaza’s population would be unacceptable. It would not only be a serious violation of international law, but also a major obstacle to the two-state solution and a destabilizing factor for our close partners, Egypt and Jordan. Gaza’s future lies with a future Palestinian state.

We reaffirm our unwavering solidarity with the families of all the hostages and with the people of Israel in the wake of the barbaric acts committed by Hamas in the worst anti-Semitic massacre since the Holocaust. Our thoughts go out in particular to the loved ones of our fellow citizen, Ohad Yahalomi, who is still in captivity.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Support for the two-state solution
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Support for the two-state solution

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Multilateralism

Multilateralism

Déclaration

On : February 05th 2025

France reiterates its opposition to any forced displacement of Gaza’s Palestinian population, which would constitute a serious violation of international law, an attack on the legitimate aspirations of Palestinians and also a major obstacle to the two-state solution and a factor of major destabilization for our close partners, Egypt and Jordan, and the whole region.

France will continue actively promoting the implementation of the two-state solution, which alone can guarantee long-term peace and security to Israelis and Palestinians. Gaza’s future must lie not in the prospect of control by a third State but in the framework of a future Palestinian State, under the aegis of the Palestinian Authority. Hamas must be disarmed and have no part in the territory’s governance. France will continue to express its opposition to settlement activity – which is contrary to international law – and to any hint of the unilateral annexation of the West Bank.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Arab League’s support for New York Declaration on the Peaceful Settlement of the Question of Palestine and the Implementation of the Two-State solution - Statement by the ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs deputy spok
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Arab League’s support for New York Declaration on the Peaceful Settlement of the Question of Palestine and the Implementation of the Two-State solution - Statement by the ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs deputy spok

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Multilateralism

Multilateralism

Déclaration

On : September 05th 2025

France welcomes the Arab League states’ unprecedented call to endorse and implement the New York Declaration, adopted following the international conference on the implementation of the two-state solution, co-chaired by France and Saudi Arabia on 28 and 29 July.

This call reflects the Arab League States’ desire to commit to the implementation of an ambitious road map for the peace and security of everyone in the region, which requires an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and the release of all the hostages, the establishment of a viable, sovereign Palestinian State, the disarmament of Hamas and its exclusion from Gaza’s governance, normalization between Israel and the Arab countries, and the implementation of collective security guarantees incorporating Israel. We call on all the States to join this road map.

title: Israel/Palestine – Armed incidents in the Gaza Strip
content:

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Conflicts

Conflicts

Déclaration

On : October 15th 2025

France strongly condemns the summary executions perpetrated by Hamas in Gaza in recent days.

With the entry into force of the ceasefire providing the opportunity to relieve Gazans’ suffering after two years of war, the armed incidents that have occurred in the past few days are especially worrying.

France asks all the parties on the ground to refrain from all actions likely to endanger the population, the viability of the ceasefire and access to humanitarian aid. It also recalls that, in accordance with the New York Declaration endorsed by 142 United Nations Member States and with President Trump’s peace plan, Hamas must have no future role in the governance of Gaza and must be disarmed.

title: Israel/Palestine – Release of hostages
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestine – Release of hostages

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

Conflicts

Conflicts

Déclaration

On : October 13th 2025

France is deeply relieved following the release this morning of the 20 living Israeli hostages held in the Gaza Strip, now finally free after more than two years of a cruel ordeal.

Its thoughts and heartfelt sympathy are with their families and close friends, who have gone through unbearable anguish and waiting during that time.

France also pays tribute to the memory of the remaining 28 hostages, who died in captivity and whose bodies are still being held. It calls for their remains to be returned immediately to their families, so that they can grieve in a dignified way.

France commends all those who have worked tirelessly for this outcome, particularly the United States and the regional mediators who made it possible for the agreement to be reached.

President Macron will take part in the peace plan’s signing ceremony in Sharm-el-Sheikh today. This ceremony will provide an opportunity to reiterate the need to achieve lasting peace in Gaza, with a view to recreating a horizon of peace after more than two years of war and suffering with the establishment of a two-state solution, the only solution enabling lasting peace to be guaranteed in the region. France supports the full implementation of this plan and is working towards practical contributions for ensuring regional stability and security.

title: Israel/Palestine – Delivery of humanitarian cargo to Gaza
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestine – Delivery of humanitarian cargo to Gaza

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

Communiqué

On : January 18th 2026

Given the immense humanitarian needs in Gaza, France has decided to dispatch nearly 400 tonnes of food aid. These Nutriset nutritional supplements will improve the health of over 42,000 malnourished Gazan children between the ages of 6 months and 2 years.

This in-kind-aid operation initiated by the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs is remarkable in its scale as much as the State/company/UN partnership that made it possible. We thank the stakeholders for their essential cooperation, along with the World Food Programme (WFP), which will take on distribution in Gaza.

This operation is part of the steadfast humanitarian commitment France has made since 7 October 2023, reflected in the delivery of over 1,300 tonnes of humanitarian cargo for civilians, through the United Nations, NGO partners and in-kind contributions.

France reiterates that it is a matter of urgency for Israel to remove all obstacles so that the United Nations and NGOs can go on delivering humanitarian aid independently and impartially throughout the Gaza Strip, in accordance with international humanitarian law and United Nations Security Council Resolution 2803.

title: Israel/Palestine – Israeli government measures regarding the West Bank
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestine – Israeli government measures regarding the West Bank

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Conflicts

Conflicts

Déclaration

On : February 10th 2026

France strongly condemns the Israeli security cabinet’s decisions aimed at expanding Israel’s control over the West Bank, particularly Areas A and B.

These decisions are contrary to international law and represent a dangerous challenge to the Oslo Accords and the Hebron Protocol.

These measures, along with the approval of the E1 project and the publication of a tender for that project, are fueling momentum for annexing the West Bank and as such, represent a serious attack on the two-state solution. At a time when international efforts are focused on implementing phase two of the Gaza peace plan, these decisions are detrimental to current peace efforts and threaten to raise tensions.

We call on Israel to immediately reverse these decisions and reaffirm our strong opposition to any form of annexation. France will remain committed to a just and lasting peace in the region in accordance with Security Council resolutions and the principles set forth in the New York Declaration.

title: Israel/Palestine – Peace plan proposed by President D. J. Trump

Hamas response to the peace plan proposed by President Trump is a significant step toward ending the war in Gaza and ensuring the immediate release of all hostages.

France calls upon all parties to implement, without delay, each and every point of the plan, the principles of which are in line with the New York Declaration.

France welcomes the Egyptian, Qatari and Turkish mediators’ efforts, as well as President Trump’s commitment, to achieving peace.

France stands ready to continue working with the United States of America, the Israeli government, the Palestinian Authority and all regional and international partners to help end the crisis in Gaza and rebuild momentum toward lasting peace in the Middle East, on the basis of this plan and the New York Declaration.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Injured Palestinian children taken in by French hospitals
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Injured Palestinian children taken in by French hospitals

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

Déclaration

On : January 16th 2025

France continues its efforts to assist civilian populations in the Gaza Strip. Yesterday, it took part in a coordinated medical evacuation operation alongside the European Union and the World Health Organization, enabling 12 Palestinian patients to leave Gaza. Six of them were welcomed in France.

Once they arrived in Paris, these six children and the 16 family members accompanying them were cared for by the Crisis and Support Center of the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs, along with the Ministry of Health. Since the start of 2024, a total of 23 Palestinian children have received treatment in French hospitals.

France expresses its gratitude to all its UN, European and international partners who made these evacuations possible.

France calls for the immediate reopening of all Gaza crossing points and for Israel to facilitate the operations of international organizations and humanitarian agencies, in line with international law and the rulings of the International Court of Justice.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Worsening of the situation in the Gaza Strip
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Worsening of the situation in the Gaza Strip

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Human rights

Human rights

Déclaration

On : July 23rd 2025

France strongly condemns the expansion of the Israeli offensive to central Gaza, where evacuation orders have led to the displacement of tens of thousands of people in Deir el-Balah and are hampering the work of many UN agencies and international NGOs.

This latest operation is accelerating the deterioration of the humanitarian situation, marked by malnutrition and the risk of famine. This situation is the result of the blockade imposed by Israel. France condemns in the strongest possible terms the Israeli shooting of Palestinians in Gaza who are seeking to obtain humanitarian aid; according to the UN, they have killed more than a thousand people in the past two months.

France once again calls on Israel to comply with the provisional measures ordered by the International Court of Justice as well as its obligations under international humanitarian law, and to immediately lift all restrictions on humanitarian aid to Gaza in order to enable its immediate, massive, unimpeded delivery.

France also asks the Israeli authorities to allow UN agencies to operate in Gaza and to stop pressuring their employees. We deplore the revocation of the visa of the Head of the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) in the Palestinian Territories. Lastly, in keeping with the Foreign Minister’s request, France calls for allowing members of the free and independent press to enter Gaza so that they can show and document what is happening there.

We offer our condolences to the victim’s family and wish the wounded individual and newborn a speedy recovery. France reiterates its emphatic condemnation of terrorism, for which there can be no justification.

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Terrorist attack in Jerusalem

Security

On : September 09th 2025

France condemns in the strongest possible terms the terrorist attack that was committed today in Jerusalem’s Ramot neighborhood, which left at least five people dead and over a dozen others injured.

France extends its condolences to the families of the victims and wishes those injured a speedy recovery.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – France’s contribution to the European EUBAM Rafah mission
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – France’s contribution to the European EUBAM Rafah mission

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

Déclaration

On : January 31st 2025

In a show of support for the ceasefire, this morning France deployed three gendarmes specialized in border policing to serve with the European Union Border Assistance Mission for the Rafah Crossing Point (EUBAM Rafah). The Mission has about 20 staffers, mostly Italian and Spanish. The teams will act as trusted third parties at the Rafah crossing point between Gaza and Egypt; they will also reinforce Palestinian Authority personnel at the crossing point, as several hundred civilians prepare to be evacuated from Gaza for the treatment of medical emergencies.

EUBAM Rafah is a civilian mission established in November 2005. It provides advice and technical assistance on integrated border management to personnel from the Palestinian General Administration for Borders and Crossings. On January 27, the EU foreign ministers meeting in Brussels decided to deploy specialized teams to Rafah at the request of the Palestinian Authority and Israel, with the agreement of Egypt.

France reaffirms its commitment to a lasting peace based on the two-State solution and reiterates its support for the Palestinian Authority, which must be able to govern throughout the Palestinian Territories, including Gaza. As President Macron announced during his State visit to Riyadh in late 2024, a conference on the implementation of the two-State solution co-chaired by Saudi Arabia will be held in New York this coming June.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Death of Mr Ahmed Shamia, PAUSE programme laureate
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Death of Mr Ahmed Shamia, PAUSE programme laureate

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Defence and security

Defence and security

Déclaration

On : May 14th 2025

France was deeply shocked to learn of the death of Mr Ahmed Shamia, very seriously wounded in an Israeli airstrike on Gaza City on 7 May 2025. It extends its sincere condolences to his family, particularly his wife.

Mr Shamia, a Palestinian architect and artist, was a PAUSE programme laureate. This programme of scientific and artistic excellence, run by the Collège de France with the support of the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs among others, allows laureates to join higher-education institutions in France. As a reminder, some 20 PAUSE laureates were evacuated by the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs through its Crisis and Support Centre on 16 and 23 April 2025.

France reiterates its call for an immediate end to the hostilities that are harming Gaza’s civilians. The Israeli authorities must guarantee the protection of all civilians and humanitarian staff, and lift all obstacles to the entry of humanitarian aid. France repeats its call for the unconditional release of all the hostages still being held in Gaza, and for the resumption of negotiations with a view to a ceasefire.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories - Joint Statement of the Ministers of Foreign Affairs of France, Germany and the United Kingdom on the situation in Gaza
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories - Joint Statement of the Ministers of Foreign Affairs of France, Germany and the United Kingdom on the situation in Gaza

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Defence and security

Defence and security

Déclaration conjointe

On : March 22nd 2025

The resumption of Israeli strikes in Gaza marks a dramatic step backward for the people of Gaza, the hostages, their families and the entire region. We are appalled by the civilian casualties and urgently call for an immediate return to a ceasefire.

We call on all parties to re-engage with negotiations to ensure the ceasefire is implemented in full and becomes permanent. This must include Hamas releasing the hostages that they have cruelly detained and persistently refuse to release.

All Israelis and Palestinians have a right to peace and security. We call on all those with influence over Hamas to use that influence to ensure no further attacks against Israel. We are clear that Hamas must neither govern Gaza nor be a threat to Israel anymore. However, this conflict cannot be resolved through military means. A return to fighting will only result in the deaths of more Palestinian civilians and Israeli hostages.

More bloodshed is in no-one’s interest. Israel should fully respect international law and allow the flow of aid immediately. Civilians should be protected and not be cut off from essential aid or assistance. We call on Israel to restore humanitarian access, including water and electricity, and ensure access to medical care and temporary medical evacuations in accordance with international humanitarian law.

We are deeply shocked by the deadly incident affecting a UNOPS building in Gaza, which has killed one UN employee and injured several others. Amongst the victims were European citizens. UN personnel and its premises should be protected and never be a target. There must be a full investigation into what happened.

A long-lasting ceasefire is the only credible pathway towards a sustainable peace, a two-state solution and the reconstruction of Gaza.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Demolition of a school in the West Bank
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Demolition of a school in the West Bank

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Human rights

Human rights

On : August 15th 2025

France strongly condemns the destruction by the Israeli authorities of a school under construction in the north of the West Bank, funded by the French Development Agency in conjunction with the European Union. We are holding the Israeli authorities to account for this demolition.

The school, being built as part of the French Development Agency’s rural development programme, was soon due to house some 100 Palestinian children.

This is the second demolition of a project financed by the French Development Agency in the West Bank, adding to the destruction of the Al-Bustan Association centre in East Jerusalem, supported by the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and 21 French local authorities.

The continuation of settlement policy constitutes a serious violation of international law and threatens the prospect of the two-state solution.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Two French nationals wounded in Israeli strikes on Gaza
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Two French nationals wounded in Israeli strikes on Gaza

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

United Nations

United Nations

On : March 21st 2025

France is deeply distressed that two French nationals employed by the UN were seriously wounded in airstrikes that hit several buildings housing UN personnel in Gaza. Those strikes also killed a UNOPS employee. We offer our fellow citizens and their families our support and solidarity and stand ready to assist them.

As the Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs stated, we reiterate our condemnation of this attack on a UN building, which is unacceptable, and we call for a swift and thorough investigation that identifies those responsible for this incident. The protection of humanitarian personnel, who have already paid a very heavy toll in Gaza, must be guaranteed in all circumstances in line with international law.

France applauds the enormous courage of humanitarian personnel who risk their lives to assist civilian populations in the Gaza Strip and throughout the world.

We will continue to stand by them and to support their work.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Crew of the Freedom Flotilla
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Crew of the Freedom Flotilla

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

Communiqué

On : June 10th 2025

Six French nationals aboard a vessel attempting to reach the Gaza Strip were apprehended at sea by Israeli security forces yesterday.

France’s Consulate General in Tel Aviv is closely following their situation and was able to pay them an initial visit as soon as they arrived on Israeli soil to ask about their conditions, their treatment and their health. A new request for a consular visit was made. Our consular post is also in touch with their loved ones.

As the Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs has reiterated, France calls on the Israeli government to allow the immediate, massive, unhindered entry of humanitarian aid into Gaza.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Humanitarian freight bound for the Gaza Strip
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Humanitarian freight bound for the Gaza Strip

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Humanitarian action

Humanitarian action

On : July 14th 2025

A plane chartered by the European Union is today transporting nearly 52 tonnes of humanitarian aid supplied by France to civilians in the Gaza Strip.

The cargo, put together by the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs’ Crisis and Support Centre, includes tents, blankets, ground sheets and tarpaulins for more than 1,000 Palestinian families in the Gaza Strip and nearly a tonne of medicines donated by the French non-profit organization Tulipe. The despatch of tents to Gaza supports the broader humanitarian efforts being conducted in particular by Jordan and Qatar.

In Amman, the shipment will be handed over to the Jordan Hashemite Charity Organization, a Jordanian partner institution, which will ensure its delivery to Gaza in line with its coordinating role in this regard. Several partners of France, including the French NGOs Première Urgence Internationale and Médecins du Monde, will ensure the aid is delivered in the Gaza Strip via Jordan’s humanitarian land corridor.

France thanks the European Union, Jordan, Qatar and all its partners which are contributing to this new solidarity operation. France remains fully mobilized to address the humanitarian emergency in Gaza. It continues to call for all access points to be opened and for the work of humanitarian actors, international organizations and nongovernmental organizations to be facilitated, in accordance with international law.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Murder of Odeh Hathalin in the West Bank
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Murder of Odeh Hathalin in the West Bank

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Counter-terrorism

Counter-terrorism

Déclaration

On : July 29th 2025

France condemns this murder in the strongest possible terms, along with all the violence that extremist settlers are deliberately inflicting on the Palestinian population and which is spreading throughout the West Bank. Kafr Malik and the Christian village of Taybeh are recent examples.

France was deeply saddened to learn of the murder of Odeh Hathalin,who represented the village of Umm al-Khair in the Masafer Yatta Valley and was a colleague of Yuval Abraham, winner of the Anna Politkovskaya – Arman Soldin Prize for Courage in Journalism.

These violent actions are acts of terrorism. Settlers have killed more than 30 people since the beginning of 2022. The Israeli authorities must uphold their responsibilities and immediately punish the perpetrators of such violence, which is continuing with impunity, and protect Palestinian civilians.

France reiterates its total condemnation of settlement activity, which is contrary to international law, as the International Court of Justice concluded in its advisory opinion of July 19, 2024. At the national level and through three European packages, we have adopted sanctions against settlers guilty of violence against Palestinian civilians in the West Bank.

We will continue to support the adoption of further measures by the EU, and if the violence continues, we stand ready to take additional measures at the national level, including sanctions against members of the government who support such violence.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories - Further extension of Israeli military operations in the Gaza Strip
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories - Further extension of Israeli military operations in the Gaza Strip

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Governance

Governance

Security

Security

On : August 08th 2025

France condemns in the strongest possible terms the plan adopted overnight by the Israeli government to further extend its military operations to take control of Gaza City in order to establish military control over the entire Gaza Strip.

France recalls that it is firmly opposed to any plans involving the occupation of the Gaza Strip and the forced displacement of its population. Such actions would constitute further serious violations of international law and would lead to complete deadlock. They would also undermine the Palestinians’ legitimate aspirations to live in peace in a viable, sovereign and contiguous State, and would threaten regional stability. They would in no way improve the security of Israel or its people, nor that of the hostages still being held by Hamas in Gaza. France calls on Hamas to immediately and unconditionally release the hostages and to take up the ceasefire proposals rather that prolong the suffering of the people of Gaza through its obstructionist attitude. Furthermore, it calls for the disarmament and surrender of Hamas.

The future of the Gaza Strip must be part of a future Palestinian State led by the Palestinian Authority. France will continue to work towards the implementation of the two-State solution, which is the only way to ensure lasting peace and security for Israelis and Palestinians, as it did at the New York Conference, which it co-chaired with Saudi Arabia. France will continue the work which began in New York, with its partners and the United Nations, to deploy a temporary international stabilization mission in order to ensure the security of Israelis and Palestinians. It calls on its partners and all other States to join these collective efforts.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – E1 settlement plan
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – E1 settlement plan

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Human rights

Human rights

Déclaration

On : August 16th 2025

France utterly condemns the Israeli authorities’ decision to validate the E1 settlement plan, which provides for the construction of more than 3,000 homes to the east of Jerusalem.

France calls on Israel to abandon the plan, which constitutes a serious violation of international law. Its implementation would cut the West Bank in two and seriously endanger the two-state solution, which alone is capable of guaranteeing lasting peace and security for Israelis and Palestinians.

France reiterates its condemnation of settlement activity and of all the tension and violence it arouses. It continues to work actively alongside its European partners to step up pressure on Israel to end settlement activity, including through new sanctions against the individuals and entities responsible for settlement activity.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – E1 settlement plan in the West Bank
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – E1 settlement plan in the West Bank

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Human rights

Human rights

Déclaration

On : July 15th 2025

France is strongly opposed to the revival of the E1 settlement plan announced by the central planning bureau of Israel’s Civil Administration. This plan, which had been frozen since 2021, provides for the construction of more than 3,000 housing units east of Jerusalem.

This plan must not be implemented. It is contrary to international law and threatens the prospect of a two-State solution.

France reiterates its condemnation of settlement activity and all the tensions and violence it elicits. We repeat that only a two-State solution can guarantee lasting peace and security for Israelis and Palestinians.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Revocation by the Israeli authorities of travel permits for two French delegations
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Revocation by the Israeli authorities of travel permits for two French delegations

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

On : April 29th 2025

The Israeli authorities’ decision to revoke travel permits for two French delegations led by associations promoting decentralized cooperation (Association pour le jumelage entre les camps de réfugiés palestiniens and Cités Unis France) and composed of several elected officials who were supposed to travel to Israel and the Palestinian Territories, is regrettable, counterproductive and damaging to French-Israeli relations.

Public accusations made by the Israeli Embassy in France regarding supposed ties between these associations and terrorist organizations are unacceptable.

France calls on the Israeli authorities to reverse these decisions, which punish those who are striving for a lasting peace between Israelis and Palestinians.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Situation in the West Bank
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Situation in the West Bank

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Defence and security

Defence and security

On : February 11th 2025

France is deeply concerned about the many civilian casualties – particularly children – and the people displaced as a result of the Israeli operations under way in the north of the West Bank.

The way the use of force is being employed by the Israeli army in the West Bank is extremely concerning. France calls on the Israeli authorities to show the utmost restraint in the use of force and to comply with their international obligations under international humanitarian law by ensuring the protection of civilians in the Occupied Palestinian Territories. Children must never be targeted.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Gaza Strip Departures
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Gaza Strip Departures

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Defence and security

Defence and security

On : April 25th 2025

France has been working for 18 months to provide refuge to its citizens and their dependents, the staff of the French Institute in Gaza and their families, and Palestinian figures with ties to our country.

On April 23, the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs organized the departure of 115 people from the Gaza Strip; they arrived in France today. This operation follows a previous one on April 16, in which 59 people were evacuated to France. Among the evacuees were a number of children, who can finally join their families living in France. This operation stems from a desire to protect our nationals and their dependents; all of the evacuees had ties to France.

France reaffirms its commitment to the right of return for Gazans in the enclave. We reiterate our opposition to any forced displacement of Gaza’s Palestinian population, which would be a serious violation of international law and a major destabilizing factor for our close partners, Egypt and Jordan, and for the entire region. It would also jeopardize diplomatic prospects for ending the conflict, which must come about through the implementation of the two-State solution, which we will champion at the French-Saudi conference in June 2025.

France reiterates its call for full, unhindered humanitarian access allowing aid for the civilian population to enter the Gaza Strip, and for the resumption of ceasefire negotiations.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Departures from the Gaza Strip
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Departures from the Gaza Strip

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Defence and security

Defence and security

Communiqué

On : July 11th 2025

France has been working actively for nearly two years to ensure the protection of its nationals and their dependants, staff of the Institut français de Gaza and their families, holders of French Government scholarships, and Palestinian figures with links to our country.

On Wednesday 9 July, the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs organized the departure of 37 people from the Gaza Strip; they returned to France today. The operation follows several conducted since January 2025, which have allowed 292 people to be evacuated to France.

France reiterates its firm opposition to any forced displacement of Gaza’s Palestinian population, which would be a serious violation of international law. It recalls its commitment to the two-state solution, the only one capable of enabling Israelis and Palestinians to live in peace and security.

France reiterates its call for full and unimpeded humanitarian access for aid to enter the Gaza Strip for the benefit of civilians, and for a durable ceasefire to be concluded.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Israeli shelling of the Holy Family Church in Gaza
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Israeli shelling of the Holy Family Church in Gaza

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Human rights

Human rights

Déclaration

On : July 18th 2025

France condemns in the strongest possible terms the unacceptable attack by the Israeli Army that struck the Holy Family Church and left three people dead and a dozen injured.

The Minister called the Latin Patriarch of Jerusalem to express France’s solidarity with Gaza’s Christian community following the attack on the Holy Family Catholic Parish, placed under France’s historic protection by the 1913 Constantinople agreement.

Every day, dozens of civilians are killed in Gaza by Israeli fire and bombardments. These attacks are unjustifiable. Civilians must never be targeted, and neither should civilian infrastructure, places of worship, hospitals or shelters in Gaza. France reiterates its call for an immediate ceasefire as well as a complete lifting of the humanitarian blockade in order the facilitate the resumption of a massive, dignified, unhindered delivery of aid for civilians by the UN and organizations that abide by international law.

title: Israel/Palestinian Territories – Israeli National Security Minister Ben-Gvir’s visit to Haram al-Sharif/Temple Mount
content:
Home

News

Israel/Palestinian Territories – Israeli National Security Minister Ben-Gvir’s visit to Haram al-Sharif/Temple Mount

Israel / Palestine

Israel / Palestine

Security

Security

On : August 04th 2025

France condemns yesterday’s visit by the Israel Minister of National Security, Itamar Ben-Gvir, to Haram al-Sharif/Temple Mount in violation of the historic Status Quo agreement on Jerusalem’s holy sites.

In Jerusalem, now systematic challenges to the Status Quo on Haram al-Sharif/Temple Mount are undermining international efforts to achieve a two-State solution, the only solution likely to fulfill the legitimate aspirations of Palestinians and Israelis to live in peace and security.

France stresses the imperative need to preserve the historic Status Quo agreement on Jerusalem’s holy sites and underscores the importance of Jordan’s specific role in this regard.

title: Japan – Commemoration of the 80th anniversary of the bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki (August 6 and 9, 2025)
content:
Home

News

Japan – Commemoration of the 80th anniversary of the bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki (August 6 and 9, 2025)

Japan

Japan

Disarmament and non-proliferation

Disarmament and non-proliferation

On : August 06th 2025

As we commemorate the 80th anniversary of the bombing of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, France remembers the victims of that tragedy. We are taking part in the peace ceremonies held in Hiroshima and Nagasaki on August 6 and 9.

France reaffirms the priority importance of a safer international environment, with progress in the areas of nuclear disarmament and non-proliferation in accordance with the goals of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons.

title: Japan – Meeting of Minister Jean-Noël Barrot with his counterpart
content:
Home

News

Japan – Meeting of Minister Jean-Noël Barrot with his counterpart

Japan

Japan

Ministers

Ministers

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

Communiqué

On : May 03rd 2025

The Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, Mr Jean-Noël Barrot, received his Japanese counterpart, Mr Iwaya Takeshi, for a working lunch in Paris on 2 May 2025.

The two Ministers discussed the strengthening of every dimension of the exceptional partnership between France and Japan, including diplomacy, security, the economy and technology, and culture, as well as deepening strategic cooperation, notably in the Indo-Pacific.

The Ministers recalled the fundamental principles and values that France and Japan share and, in the context of major geopolitical instability, underlined the crucial importance of promoting and enforcing a rules-based international order and multilateral cooperation. They affirmed the shared will of France and Japan to enhance their resilience and their respective sovereignty. The two Ministers also talked about the global economic situation.

They also discussed major crises and situations around the world, including Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine, the situation in the Middle East and the latest developments on the Korean Peninsula. They reaffirmed their commitment to the sovereignty, territorial integrity and independence of Ukraine. The Minister welcomed Japan’s resolute support for Ukraine and assured his counterpart of France’s full solidarity in the face of North Korean actions.

He reaffirmed the importance of cooperation with Japan to prepare for major international events such as the third United Nations Conference to Support the Implementation of Sustainable Development Goal 14: Conserve and sustainably use the oceans, seas and marine resources for sustainable development (UNOC3, or UN Ocean Conference), to be held in Nice in June 2025, and France’s 2026 G7 Presidency. The Minister also congratulated Japan on the successful opening and visitor numbers of the World Expo in Osaka.

title: Jordan – Conversation between Jean-Noël Barrot and his Jordanian counterpart
content:
Home

News

Jordan – Conversation between Jean-Noël Barrot and his Jordanian counterpart

Jordan

Jordan

Ministers

Ministers

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

Communiqué

On : February 14th 2025

Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs Jean-Noël Barrot spoke today with his Jordanian counterpart, Ayman Safadi.

During their conversation, and following on from the Paris Conference on Syria held on February 13, the two Ministers addressed the situation in Syria. They agreed on the importance of supporting a just, inclusive political transition that fulfills the aspirations of the Syrian people and respects the rights of all Syrians.

They also reaffirmed their shared desire to work to preserve the ceasefire in Gaza, ensure the release of all hostages, rebuild Gaza and secure a lasting peace based on the two-State solution – the only thing capable of guaranteeing the Israelis’ and Palestinians’ legitimate aspirations for peace and security. The two Ministers reiterated their strong opposition to any forced displacement of Gaza’s Palestinian population.

title: Day of International Criminal Justice
content:
Home

News

Day of International Criminal Justice

International justice

International justice

Communiqué

On : July 17th 2025

On this Day of International Criminal Justice, which marks the anniversary of the adoption of the treaty that established the International Criminal Court, France reaffirms its staunch commitment to the worldwide fight against impunity.

France reiterates its commitment to promoting and protecting international law, of which the ICC – the only permanent, universal international court – is a linchpin. It has pledged to promote this Court’s impendence, impartiality, effectiveness and universality.

We commend the work carried out by the International Residual Mechanism for Criminal Tribunals, which has made it possible to prosecute and judge the perpetrators of genocide, crimes against humanity, and serious violations of international humanitarian law in Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia. We recognize the vital contribution of hybrid courts and tribunals – among them the Kosovo Specialist Chambers, the Special Criminal Court in the Central African Republic and their predecessors – to the fight against impunity. These international courts, which work alongside national courts, help meet our shared goal of obtaining justice for the victims of the most serious crimes, preventing those crimes and supporting the reconciliation process.

title: International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia and Transphobia
content:
Home

News

International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia and Transphobia

LGBTQ rights

LGBTQ rights

Déclaration

On : May 16th 2025

On the eve of the International Day Against Homophobia, Biphobia and Transphobia, France reaffirms the urgent need for a worldwide fight against the ongoing persecution, discrimination and violence against LGBT+ people.

France reiterates its call for the universal decriminalization of homosexuality and for the fundamental rights of all LGBT+ people to be fully respected. Human rights, especially the right to a private life and the right to be free from discrimination, are universal and must extend to everyone, in all countries.

As a pioneer in defending the rights of LGBT+ people, in 2022 France appointed an ambassador who carries these messages to national governments, the EU, international organizations and civil society. We have established a specific fund to support rights defenders and provide assistance to LGBT+ people who are in danger.

In an international climate in which the rights of LGBT+ people are all too often challenged, they have never been more of a priority for France’s human rights diplomacy. In multilateral fora and in its bilateral relations, France champions these rights, in the name of the universality of human rights. We are working actively within the UN as part of the Equal Rights Coalition (ERC) and the UN LGBTI Core Group.

We will support the European Commission as it updates its strategy on the rights of LGBT+ people and will emphasize the fight against harassment and violence, including online.

We will support the renewal of the term of the Independent Expert on Protection against Violence and Discrimination based on Sexual Orientation and Gender Identity at the Human Rights Council this July. We applaud the UN’s 2024 adoption of an inter-agency strategy on this issue.

title: International Day to End Impunity for Crimes against Journalists
content:
Home

News

International Day to End Impunity for Crimes against Journalists

Freedom of expression

Freedom of expression

On : November 02nd 2025

On International Day to End Impunity for Crimes against Journalists, which France initiated at the United Nations General Assembly in 2013, we reaffirm our steadfast commitment to press freedom and the protection of journalists all over the world.

In an international context of increasing attacks on the freedom to inform, and of disinformation and growing pressures on independent media organizations, France pays tribute to those who risk their lives to defend everyone’s basic right to free, plural and reliable information.

France praises the courage of all those doing their job in difficult conditions, in particular in conflict zones, with a special thought for the family and close friends of photojournalist Antoni Lallican, killed on 3 October 2025 during a Russian drone strike while he was covering the war of aggression waged by Russia against Ukraine since 2022. France utterly condemns this attack and asks that full light be shed on the circumstances of his death.

The fight against impunity for crimes committed against journalists remains a priority of French diplomatic action. France fully supports the United Nations’ mechanisms, particularly the UN Plan of Action on the Safety of Journalists and the Issue of Impunity, and calls for its enhanced implementation. It also supports the initiatives of the International Partnership for Information and Democracy launched in 2019, and the Council of Europe’s Platform to promote the protection of journalism and the safety of journalists.

At European level, France is working actively for the implementation of the European Media Freedom Act adopted in 2024, which aims to bolster editorial independence, media transparency and the protection of journalists against political and economic pressures.

France will continue its resolute action with its European and international partners to guarantee the safety of journalists, particularly women journalists, independent journalists in authoritarian States, and reporters in theatres of operation.

It will continue to champion, throughout the world, the right to inform, be informed and express critical opinions, both online and offline – conditions essential for democratic debate and lasting peace.

title: Kyrgyzstan/Tajikistan – Signing of a border delimitation treaty
content:
Home

News

Kyrgyzstan/Tajikistan – Signing of a border delimitation treaty

Kyrgyzstan

Kyrgyzstan

Tajikistan

Tajikistan

Multilateralism

Multilateralism

Déclaration

On : March 14th 2025

France welcomes the signing in Bishkek on 13 March of the final border delimitation agreement between the Kyrgyz Republic and the Republic of Tajikistan.

The announcement, which comes at the end of a long process, sends an important signal for stability and the continued regional integration of the five countries of Central Asia. The reopening of the borders is an essential stage in strengthening human and economic exchanges.

In this same spirit of normalizing relations between Central Asian countries, France would like to welcome the upcoming trilateral summit in Khujand on 31 March between the Uzbek, Tajik and Kyrgyz presidents.

title: Kosovo – Unilateral closure of Serbian “parallel institutions”
content:
Home

News

Kosovo – Unilateral closure of Serbian “parallel institutions”

Kosovo

Kosovo

Governance

Governance

Déclaration

On : January 15th 2025

France regrets the Kosovo government’s unilateral closure of numerous Serbian parallel institutions on January 15. This decision will have a negative impact on the daily lives of Kosovo’s Serbian population and undermines ongoing efforts to normalize relations between Kosovo and Serbia through the EU-led dialogue.

This unilateral action threatens the prospects of lifting restrictive measures imposed on Kosovo. France calls for all parties to honor their commitments in order to move forward with the implementation of the Ohrid Agreement.

title: Kuwait - Visit of Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs
content:
Home

News

Kuwait - Visit of Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs

Kuwait

Kuwait

Ministers

Ministers

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

Communiqué

On : April 25th 2025

Mr. Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, paid an official visit to Kuwait on Thursday, 24 April 2025, as part of his Middle-East trip

He was received by the Crown Prince of Kuwait, His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Khaled Al-Hamad Al-Sabah, and by the Acting Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, His Excellency Sheikh Fahad Al-Youssef Al-Sabah. On this occasion, the Minister praised the long-standing and deep friendship between the two countries, which spans the diplomatic, economic, cultural, military, educational, academic, research, and health fields. He welcomed the prospect of an official visit to Paris this year by His Highness the Amir Sheikh Meshal Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah and His Highness the Crown Prince, at the invitation of the President of the Republic.

In order to strengthen the partnership between France and Kuwait, the Minister and his counterpart, His Excellency Abdullah Ali Al-Yahya, signed a declaration of intent on energy cooperation as well as an intergovernmental agreement on sharing and exchange of classified information in the defense field.

The Minister congratulated Kuwait on its presidency of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and expressed his wish to strengthen coordination between the two countries in response to regional crises.

Faced with the scale of destruction in Gaza, they rejected any solution involving the forced displacement of the Palestinian population, which would constitute a grave breach of international law. The Minister reaffirmed France’s commitment to a ceasefire, the release of all hostages, and negotiations toward a credible “day after” plan, in line with the proposals in the Arab plan for Gaza. He recalled that France and Saudi Arabia will co-chair an international conference in the summer in support of the two-state solution, the only one capable of ensuring lasting peace.

Regarding Syria, the Minister reiterated France’s commitment to a rigorous political transition that respects Syrian pluralism and addresses collective security challenges, especially the fight against terrorism.

On Lebanon, the Minister expressed France’s deep concern over renewed tensions in the south and called on all parties to respect the ceasefire agreement reached in November 2024.

The ministers also discussed global issues, particularly the protection of oceans. The Minister emphasized the need for both countries to mobilize ahead of the 3rd United Nations Ocean Conference (UNOC3), which will take place in Nice from 9 to 13 June 2025 and be co-chaired by France and Costa Rica.

Accompanied by a delegation of French business leaders, the Minister met with the Kuwaiti Minister of Finance, Economy and Investment, in the presence of the Executive Director of the Kuwait Investment Authority, and the Minister of Public Works, the Minister of Municipalities, and the Minister of Electricity. The Minister welcomed Kuwait’s ambitions for economic transformation. He highlighted the readiness of French companies to play a full part, drawing on their world-renowned expertise, particularly in the fields of transport, sustainable cities, and energy transition.

title: The EU-Mercosur agreement has to be complemented in order to effectively protect the agricultural sector and uphold European standards - Joint statement by France and Poland
content:
Home

News

The EU-Mercosur agreement has to be complemented in order to effectively protect the agricultural sector and uphold European standards - Joint statement by France and Poland

Poland

Poland

Economic diplomacy

Economic diplomacy

Déclaration conjointe

On : July 07th 2025

France and Poland call for the EU-Mercosur agreement to be complemented in order to effectively protect the agricultural sector and uphold European standards.

France and Poland are thus aligned on the need to find a better balance within the Mercosur agreement between promoting the free trade to which the European Union is committed and safeguarding Europe’s strategic interests including food security and food safety.

France and Poland reaffirm their support for fair and reciprocal free trade, and for sustainable trade agreements, which are key drivers of growth, market access, and international influence for our companies.

However, Poland and France consider that, as it currently stands, this agreement does not fulfil the necessary conditions to protect European farmers from market disruption risks and to sustainably secure the continent’s food sovereignty.

Mr Haddad and Mr Szłapka share the view that a specific agricultural safeguard clause for the sensitive products should be included in the EU-Mercosur agreement, to enable a swift and effective response — based on clear criteria — in the event of a market disruption threat or proven harm to our sectors,at the level of one or more Member States.

Furthermore, the EU-Mercosur agreement does not sufficiently guarantee the protection of our sanitary, environmental, animal welfare and social standards, nor does it ensure the level playing field among producers that we strongly advocate. This is why it is equally essential for the European Union to effectively implement mirror measures and rigorous sanitary and phytosanitary controls, which are vital to ensure fair competition and protect consumers.

The partners are convinced that European solidarity will be crucial to reaching an agreement that benefits all parties.

title: La fabrique de la diplomatie [the diplomacy factory] (5-6 september 2025) - Joint communique issued by the ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and Sorbonne nouvelle university
content:
Home

News

La fabrique de la diplomatie [the diplomacy factory] (5-6 september 2025) - Joint communique issued by the ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and Sorbonne nouvelle university

Ministry news

Ministry news

On : August 26th 2025

The Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and Sorbonne Nouvelle University are pleased to be involved in the organization of La Fabrique de la Diplomatie, a landmark event at the start of the diplomatic year on 5 and 6 September 2025.

The first La Fabrique de la Diplomatie is taking place at Sorbonne Nouvelle University’s Nation campus. Organized with the Diplomatic and Consular Academy over two days and in more than 150 specially equipped spaces, the event will allow the general public to take part in over 100 hours of debates, in games simulating negotiations and in meetings with diplomats who have very varied backgrounds and experiences.

Entry is unrestricted and free of charge, and no registration is required.

Showcasing the richness and diversity of careers geared to international affairs, La Fabrique de la Diplomatie is primarily aimed at younger generations, to allow them to get hands-on experience of the machinery of foreign policy in direct contact with all those who put it into practice.

La Fabrique de la Diplomatie, hosted by the Ministry and Sorbonne Nouvelle University, can therefore rely on unprecedented efforts by all our foreign-policy stakeholders: institutional players, international organizations, the private sector, the academic world, civil society, artists, and those implementing France’s external action.

For anyone interested in international relations, whether you are a novice or an enthusiast, the Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs and Sorbonne Nouvelle University invite you to join them at 8 avenue de Saint-Mandé, Paris 12, on 5 and 6 September.

Click on this link for a detailed programme of the two days of diplomatic immersion: rub66009

Practical information:

When: Friday 5 and Saturday 6 September 2025, from 9.00 a.m. to 8.00 p.m. Where: Nation Campus, Sorbonne Nouvelle University, 8 avenue de Saint-Mandé, 75012, Paris. How to get there: Nation metro station (RER A, lines 1, 2, 6, 9); Picpus metro station (line 6); Buses 56, 86. On-site catering: Crous (meals €1 for students).

title: France strongly condemns Russian actors and their intermediaries implicated in the Storm-1516 manoeuvres
content:
Home

News

France strongly condemns Russian actors and their intermediaries implicated in the Storm-1516 manoeuvres

Russia

Russia

Digital diplomacy

Digital diplomacy

Déclaration

On : May 06th 2025

VIGINUM is today publishing a report documenting a significant number of information operations by Russian actors using the attack group Storm-1516.

Since 2023, this cyber attack group has been used in 77 Russian information campaigns targeting France, Ukraine and all Western countries. The attack group created by Russia as part of its information war uses especially complex, sometimes high-profile distribution patterns, particularly by interfering in electoral ballots. This was the case in particular during the early general election in France in June 2024.

The Storm-1516 attack group is reportedly linked to a complex network of individuals and organizations acting from Russian Federation territory, some of whom are particularly close to the Russian Government, which suggests close links with the propaganda machine deployed by Russia.

In the face of the threat which this cyber attack group represents for French and European public debate online, France strongly condemns Russia’s destabilizing activities. This conduct is unworthy of a permanent member of the United Nations Security Council. No attempt at manipulation will dissuade France from supporting Ukraine against Russia’s war of aggression.

The social-media platforms affected by these operations have been informed and must shoulder their responsibility in the fight against this foreign digital interference, in the framework of implementing the European Union Digital Services Act.

title: France to take over the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA) presidency in 2027
content:
Home

News

France to take over the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA) presidency in 2027

Human rights

Human rights

Communiqué

On : December 19th 2025

On December 18, 2025, at the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance (IHRA) Plenary in Jerusalem, the organization’s Member Countries unanimously approved France’s bid for the IHRA presidency beginning in 2027. France will succeed Argentina in the position.

The IHRA, an intergovernmental organization comprised of 35 Member Countries and eight Observer Countries, brings together governments and experts in order to strengthen and promote education, remembrance and research on the Holocaust and the genocide of the Roma.

Against the backdrop of constant attempts to distort history and the dramatic resurgence of anti-Semitism throughout the world, France recalls that Holocaust denial and anti-Semitism must be combatted and firmly punished because they undermine the unity of our society, as well as the rights, freedoms and dignity of all.

In keeping with France’s ongoing efforts to prevent anti-Semitism and preserve the memory of the Holocaust, our country continues to work every day to defend human rights and combat all forms of anti-Semitism, racism and discrimination.

title: Strong upturn in french foreign trade in the third quarter of 2025: a decisive contribution to growth
content:
Home

News

Strong upturn in french foreign trade in the third quarter of 2025: a decisive contribution to growth

France

France

Economic diplomacy

Economic diplomacy

On : November 03rd 2025

The results published by INSEE [French National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies] confirm the strong progress in French foreign trade in the third quarter of 2025.

In an international context that is still uncertain, French exports are up 2.2% compared to the previous quarter, while imports have decreased 0.4%. Foreign trade is therefore making a positive contribution of 0.9 of a percentage point to GDP growth, after two quarters where it fell back.

Nicolas Forissier, Minister Delegate for Foreign Trade and Economic Attractiveness, says: “These results demonstrate the vitality of our economy internationally and our companies’ ability to win new contracts despite geopolitical tensions. The momentum of French exports supports growth and strengthens France’s place among the major European trading powers.”

The growth in exports of transport equipment, particularly in the aerospace sector, and the good performance of the chemical and pharmaceutical industries illustrate the dynamism of our sectors of excellence. These results reflect the practical effects of long-standing policies aimed at strengthening competitiveness and supporting businesses with their exports.

"We must continue this collective mobilization,” emphasizes the Minister. “In the face of economic and geopolitical uncertainties, it’s more essential than ever to support our strategic industries and guarantee conditions for fair global competition. Foreign trade is a major lever in terms of sovereignty and prosperity for our country.”

While the momentum is encouraging, Jean-Noël Barrot, Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs, and Nicolas Forissier, Minister Delegate for Foreign Trade and Economic Attractiveness, remain mindful of the persistent challenges, particularly in the agricultural and agrifood industries, which are facing trade tensions and declining trade balances.

title: Joint Statement by the UK, Sweden, France, Germany and The Netherlands on Alexei Navalny’s death
content:
Home

News

Joint Statement by the UK, Sweden, France, Germany and The Netherlands on Alexei Navalny’s death

Russia

Russia

Human rights

Human rights

Communiqué

On : February 14th 2026

The UK, Sweden, France, Germany and The Netherlands are confident that Alexei Navalny was poisoned with a lethal toxin.

This is the conclusion of our Governments based on analyses of samples from Alexei Navalny. These analyses have conclusively confirmed the presence of epibatidine.

Epibatidine is a toxin found in poison dart frogs in South America. It is not found naturally in Russia.

Russia claimed that Navalny died of natural causes. But given the toxicity of epibatidine and reported symptoms, poisoning was highly likely the cause of his death. Navalny died while held in prison, meaning Russia had the means, motive and opportunity to administer this poison to him.

Russia’s repeated disregard for international law and the Chemical Weapons Convention is clear.

In August 2020 the UK, Sweden, France, Germany, The Netherlands and partners condemned Russia’s use of novichok to poison Alexei Navalny.

This followed Russia’s use of novichok in Salisbury in 2018, which led to the tragic death of a British woman, Dawn Sturgess.

In both cases, only the Russian state had the combined means, motive and disregard for international law to carry out the attacks.

These latest findings once again underline the need to hold Russia accountable for its repeated violations of the Chemical Weapons Convention and, in this instance, the Biological and Toxin Weapons Convention.

Our Permanent Representatives to the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons have written today to the Director General to inform him of this Russian breach of the Chemical Weapons Convention.

We are further concerned that Russia did not destroy all of its chemical weapons.

We and our partners will make use of all policy levers at our disposal to continue to hold Russia to account.

title: Lebanon – Attack on UNIFIL
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Attack on UNIFIL

Lebanon

Lebanon

United Nations

United Nations

Security

Security

Déclaration

On : March 16th 2026

France strongly condemns the shots fired several times at soldiers of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) yesterday. These attacks from non-state armed groups are unacceptable.

France calls on all the parties to show restraint, return to the ceasefire of 26 November 2024 and comply with Security Council Resolution 1701. Hezbollah must end its operations and hand in its weapons. Israel must abandon its ground offensive, end its massive strikes and respect Lebanon’s sovereignty and territorial integrity.

UNIFIL must be able to carry out its mandate to the full and exercise its freedom of movement. The protection of the Blue Helmets and the safety and security of personnel, property and premises of the United Nations must be ensured, in accordance with international law and Resolution 1701.

title: Lebanon – Attack on a United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) convoy
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Attack on a United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) convoy

Lebanon

Lebanon

United Nations

United Nations

Communiqué

On : February 15th 2025

France condemns in the strongest terms the unacceptable attack on a vehicle belonging to the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon during the night of 14-15 February, during which the force’s deputy commander was injured. France expresses its support to him and wishes him a swift recovery.

France calls on the Lebanese security forces to ensure the safety of the blue helmets, and on the Lebanese justice system to investigate this unacceptable attack and prosecute the perpetrators.

France recalls that the protection of peacekeeping troops and the safety and security of United Nations staff, assets and property must be guaranteed.

Any attack against the UNIFIL peacekeepers is a violation of international law and Resolution 1701. We reiterate that attacks against peacekeepers may be considered war crimes.

France commends the bravery, professionalism and continued commitment of UNIFIL to foster peace and stability in Lebanon, and maintain the ceasefire with Israel.

title: Lebanon – Fifth anniversary of the Beirut port explosion
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Fifth anniversary of the Beirut port explosion

Lebanon

Lebanon

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

On : August 04th 2025

Five years after the Beirut port explosion on August 4, 2020, France remembers the victims of that tragic event and reiterates its solidarity with their loved ones. The causes of that explosion, which plunged thousands of Lebanese into mourning, have yet to be fully investigated.

In line with the commitment made by President Macron, France stands with the Lebanese authorities to assist them in their quest for truth and justice. The investigation opened by the Lebanese judiciary must be fully and independently completed. In this regard, France welcomes the Lebanese Parliament’s recent adoption of a bill designed to promote the independence of the Lebanese judiciary, a priority supported by France, which has provided human and financial assistance for that purpose.

France will continue to staunchly support Lebanon and its people in their quest for justice and sovereignty.

title: Lebanon – Election of a president
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Election of a president

Lebanon

Lebanon

Governance

Governance

Déclaration

On : January 09th 2025

France is delighted that a president has been elected in Lebanon, following the parliamentary session of 9 January. It offers its warm congratulations to Mr Joseph Aoun. It wishes him every success in carrying out his mission, which commits him at a historic, crucial moment for Lebanon’s future.

This election ends two years that have weakened Lebanon, when the presidency has been vacant, and opens a new chapter for the Lebanese people. It must provide grounds for hope for them and all Lebanon’s partners and friends, committed alongside it for years. It is encouraging for France, which has played a major role in getting Lebanon’s institutions back up and running through the active efforts of minister Jean-Noël Barrot and the good offices mission headed since June 2023 by President Macron’s personal representative to Lebanon, M. Jean-Yves Le Drian, in close conjunction with our Quintet partners.

France urgently calls on all the Lebanese political leaders and Lebanese authorities to commit to the country’s lasting recovery. This election must now be followed by the appointment of a strong government, in support of the President, capable of bringing Lebanese people together, addressing their aspirations and needs, and conducting the reforms necessary to Lebanon’s economic recovery, stability, security and sovereignty.

The election of a president will contribute to this stability and, in the immediate term, to the proper implementation of the ceasefire reached between Lebanon and Israel on 26 November 2024. France is resolutely committed to this, alongside the United States and the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon.

France has always stood by Lebanon and the Lebanese people. It will continue to do so, as the new Lebanese President, Mr Joseph Aoun, knows.

title: Lebanon – Government endorsement of the plan to restore a state monopoly on weapons
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Government endorsement of the plan to restore a state monopoly on weapons

Lebanon

Lebanon

Governance

Governance

Security

Security

On : September 06th 2025

France welcomes the Lebanese government’s endorsement of the plan proposed by the Lebanese army to re-establish the state monopoly on weapons throughout the country’s territory. This is a positive new step, in line with the Lebanese government’s decision of 5 August.

The Minister spoke with his Lebanese counterpart, Mr. Youssef Raggi, on 5 September, to underscore France’s backing for the Lebanese government’s efforts.

France calls on all Lebanese stakeholders to support an immediate and peaceful rollout of the plan, so as to deliver a stable, sovereign, rebuilt and prosperous Lebanon, with guaranteed territorial integrity, within borders agreed with its neighbours, with whom it can share peaceful relations.

Alongside its European, American and regional partners, France shall continue to stand by the Lebanese authorities as they implement their commitments, through its participation in the November 2024 ceasefire monitoring mechanism, its support for the Lebanese armed forces and its contribution to the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL). France stands ready to organise two conferences in support of the Lebanese armed forces and of recovery and reconstruction when conditions allow.

France also reiterates its call for Israel to fully respect the commitments that it undertook as part of the 26 November 2024 ceasefire agreement and to withdraw from South Lebanon.

title: Lebanon – Jean-Noël Barrot’s meeting with his counterpart
content:
Home

News

Lebanon – Jean-Noël Barrot’s meeting with his counterpart

Lebanon

Lebanon

Ministers

Ministers

Bilateral relations

Bilateral relations

Communiqué

On : February 13th 2025

The Minister for Europe and Foreign Affairs had a meeting on 13 February with his new Lebanese counterpart, Mr Youssef Raggi, on the sidelines of the Paris international conference on Syria. He warmly congratulated him on his appointment to the government that Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam formed on 8 February.

The Minister reaffirmed France’s full support for the process of change begun by President Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, aimed at putting Lebanon back on the path of reconstruction, recovery and stability. The two ministers agreed on the importance of the anticipated reforms for meeting the Lebanese people’s aspirations, for justice in particular, and enabling the country’s economic recovery.

The Minister recalled that France is resolutely committed to honouring the commitments made by Israel and Lebanon under the ceasefire of 26 November 2024. The ministers stressed the need for a complete withdrawal of the Israeli army to south of the Blue Line, in accordance with the agreed timeframe, and for Lebanon’s armed forces to be actually deployed in that area, in conformity with the United Nations Security Council resolutions.

The two ministers discussed the situation in Syria. They agreed on the need for a peaceful, inclusive transition in Syria, and for everyone’s security interests and rights to be safeguarded. In this context, they highlighted the importance of peaceful relations between Syria and Lebanon for regional stability.

Finally, they welcomed the unshakeable friendship between France and Lebanon, and the depth and diversity of ties between the Lebanese and French peoples. France will remain fully mobilized alongside the Lebanese people to support them on the path to sovereignty and prosperity.

The Minister spoke to Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam. He expressed to him France’s solidarity in the face of the ordeal Lebanon is going through as a result of Hezbollah’s irresponsible decision to join in the Iranian attacks against Israel.

He signalled France’s support for the decisions taken by the Council of Ministers to put an end to Hezbollah’s military operations. He called for the Lebanese authorities to implement these essential decisions without further delay, to protect Lebanon’s sovereignty and stability.

He emphasized France’s desire for infrastructure and civilians to be protected and for Lebanon’s territorial integrity to be respected.

France welcomes the formation of the new Lebanese government, less than a month after the election of President Joseph Aoun. It wishes the new government every success in accomplishing its mission, on which it is embarking at a historic and crucial moment for Lebanon’s future.

France will stand alongside Lebanon to help it face up to the many challenges posed to the new government. It stands ready to support it in implementing the reforms necessary to Lebanon’s economic recovery, stability and security, guaranteeing its sovereignty and contributing to its reconstruction.

title: Lebanon – Israeli airstrike on Beirut
content:
